headline: National Identity Card Race Field Determines Access to 47 Programmes
context: Analysis by the Centre for Public Policy Studies found that the race category on the MyKad identity card determines eligibility for 47 government programmes, from housing to business licences.

cards.big: Your MyKad race category determines access to 47 government programmes.
cards.sub: Including housing discounts, business licences, education scholarships, and government contracts.

cards.big: Race on MyKad is assigned at birth based on father's race.
cards.sub: It cannot be changed. A mixed-race individual is permanently categorised by patrilineal descent.

cards.big: 12 racial categories exist on MyKad — but only 1 triggers Bumiputera benefits.
cards.sub: Orang Asli, who are constitutionally indigenous, are coded separately and receive fewer benefits than Malay Bumiputera.

cards.big: Estonia abolished ethnicity on national ID in 2015.
cards.sub: Government services are delivered based on income and residency. Malaysia is one of 23 countries retaining racial ID.

cards.big: When a card you receive at birth determines lifelong access to services, identity is destiny — not choice.
Administrative race classification perpetuates the system it was designed to measure. Removing race from MyKad would not end affirmative action — it would force it to use better criteria.
